_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to improve the performance of cyberspace operators by providing constructive (positive and negative) feedback.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating AI test results to technical and non-technical stakeholders, including limitation documentation and clear go/no-go recommendations.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AG-A-002 (AG, cosine: 0.367), RC-T-008 (RC, cosine: 0.361).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.254). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, constructive, cyberspace, feedback, improve, negative] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, clear, communicating, documentation, go, limitation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.879, R=0.861). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to improve the performance of cyberspace operators by providing construc" and "Skill in communicating AI test results to technical and non-technical stakeholde" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.418 > STS 0.254 (gap +0.165). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, constructive, cyberspace, feedback, improve, negative]. WIDAI terms: [ai, clear, communicating, documentation, go, limitation].
